mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real short-term assets combined with stable consumer prices indicate strong household liquidity without inflationary pressure, supporting equity valuations and producing positive forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict direction when RSAFS increases and CPIAUCSL growth remains below 2% annually, yielding a negative Sharpe ratio on the strategy.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals expanding corporate output capacity and improving profit margins, which translates to positive S&P 500 returns over the following week during economic expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration correlates negatively with 5-day forward returns during expansion phases, or the signal loses predictive power when GDP growth exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects robust aggregate demand and corporate earnings expansion, directly driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid monthly inflation spikes trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, increasing discount rates and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 70% of months where CPIAUCSL MoM growth exceeds 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm genuine labor market tightening, boosting consumer spending forecasts and generating positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal produces negative forward returns when payroll growth exceeds 200k but unemployment fails to decline.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate strengthening residential investment and broader economic confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter during low-rate environments.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to predict positive 60-day returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5%, or the signal reverses direction during housing market corrections.
